Enjoy :)

Title: Want Me
Comment:
“From your Spotify artists I think you’ll like this song” with a link to this was the opening line from a tinder match. They then sent me an excellent playlist called “shaved just in case”. 

We exchanged like 3 messages and never met (his music was more interesting than he was). Though it’s been nearly three years, he regularly updates that playlist. It continues to slap and is where I still get a considerable amount of my new music - a gift that keeps on giving! Thanks Fred, wherever you are!

Title: The Place Where He Inserted the Blade
Comment: This was my most listened to song of 2022 and my top 5 favorite song of all time. Yes it’s 7 minutes. Yes the first two minutes are instrumental. And yes, it’s cathartic, heartbreaking, haunting, raw… and about making soup. Please at least listen to the last 2:30 seconds if can’t listen to the whole thing (looking at you Stuart) and enjoy the experience : )

Title: The World Is Yours
Comment:
Driving at night makes the world feel so small, something about the silence and emptiness. And it makes me feel like I can do anything and the world is in the palm of my hand for a few measly hours. 

This song never fails to make me feel that, but man, especially when I’m in the car driving at night. It hits even harder. 

Pete rocks’s production & sampling and Nas’s flow 😩👌🏽 for 5 minutes it feels like the world is in your hands

Title: I swear, I Really Wanted To Make A "Rap" Album But This Is Literally The Way The Wind Blew Me This Time
Comment: Rapper turned flautist!! There's a great conversation between 3-stacks and Rick Rubin about how while he (A3) was on tour and performing for outkast, he would disconnect from his performance and start to think about what he was going to eat that night, and at that point he knew he had to step away from his work because it started to mean less to him. Always respected that. Also be thankful, this is one of the shorter tracks on the album...

Title: Unison
Comment: This whole album feels like the cover. Mysterious and enchanting. Truly a unique artist. She really experiments with micro sounds, vocal storytelling  and pushes what pop could be, and this song is just beautiful to me. That a true form of love can be felt and embraced without inhibition. It’s the perfect climax to this album but also works on its own. She and this album isn’t everyone’s cup of tea but I hope you can appreciate her experimentation and boldness.
